description_long: This object is a set of three hand tools, each featuring a robust wooden handle and a heavy, dark metal head. The set includes a classic double-bit axe with a broad, symmetrical blade on both sides, a sledgehammer with a thick, rectangular striking face, and a pickaxe with a pointed end opposite a flat chisel edge. The handles are slightly curved for ergonomic grip and appear to be made of polished hardwood, showing a natural brown color. The metal heads are matte black or dark gray, with a subtle sheen and no visible decorative elements. These tools are commonly found in urban garages, maintenance sheds, or construction sites for chopping, breaking, or digging tasks.
description: A set of three hand tools with wooden handles and dark metal heads: a double-bit axe, a sledgehammer, and a pickaxe. Each tool features a sturdy, slightly curved handle and a robust, functional head.
description_view_0: A direct end-on view showing the narrow profile of the tool heads, with the handle extending downward and the blade or striking face barely visible.
description_view_1: A side view displaying all three tools: the pickaxe with its pointed and chisel ends, the sledgehammer with a rectangular head, and the double-bit axe with symmetrical blades.
description_view_2: A direct end-on view from the opposite side, again showing the narrow profile of the tool heads and the length of the handles.
description_view_3: A side view from the reverse angle, highlighting the double-bit axe's symmetrical blades, the sledgehammer's blocky head, and the pickaxe's pointed and flat ends.
attribute_handle_type: Slightly curved, cylindrical wooden handles with a smooth finish and ergonomic grip, oval cross-section.
attribute_blade_design: Double-bit axe with symmetrical curved blades, sledgehammer with rectangular striking face, pickaxe with pointed and chisel ends; all heads are robust and functional with no decorative features.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with the metal head fitted onto the wooden handle.
